title: torch-npu version must match torch major.minor version to avoid import-time FILE_LIKE mismatch
symptom: ImportError at import time: cannot import name 'FILE_LIKE' from 'torch.serialization' — installed torch version (e.g., 2.11.0) is API-incompatible with torch-npu (e.g., 2.2.0 or 2.5.1). Issue manifests at runtime, not during pip install.
root_cause: Installing torch-npu from requirements.txt without pinning the matching torch version causes pip to resolve the latest available torch, which may have API changes incompatible with the installed torch-npu release. The FILE_LIKE symbol was removed or renamed in newer torch versions. This mismatch is invisible during pip install since torch-npu does not declare a strict torch version constraint.
fix_steps: 1. Query the installed system torch-npu major.minor version: `pip show torch-npu | grep Version` (e.g., 2.5.1).
2. Pin torch to that exact version in requirements.txt: `torch==2.5.1`.
3. Add `--extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu` to pip install commands to resolve CPU-only torch wheels instead of CUDA builds.
4. Pin torchvision to the version matching the pinned torch release (e.g., `torchvision==0.20.1+cpu` for torch 2.5.1 — consult PyTorch version compatibility matrix).
5. After `pip install -r requirements.txt`, run `pip check` to catch any remaining version conflicts before runtime.
6. Validate import succeeds: `python -c 'import torch; import torch_npu'` must exit with code 0.
affected_patterns: requirements.txt with torch-npu listed but no pinned torch version
pip install -r requirements.txt without --extra-index-url for CPU torch
Assuming pip install success means environment is valid — mismatch only surfaces at import time
antipatterns: Do NOT let pip auto-resolve torch version alongside torch-npu — it will pick the latest incompatible release.
Do NOT rely on pip install exit code as validation — the version mismatch only surfaces at Python import time.
Do NOT install CUDA torch builds on NPU machines — always use --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu.
Do NOT attempt to patch torch.serialization at runtime — the correct fix is version pinning at install time.

meta.refiner_reasoning: This is a dependency resolution issue, not a code change. Phase 2 venv creation took 1144.8s due to 3 resolution cycles, confirming pip struggled with unconstrained torch-torch-npu compatibility. The fix is entirely at the requirements.txt and pip install level — no source code modifications needed.

problem_description: Installing torch-npu from requirements.txt without pinning the matching torch version causes pip to resolve the latest torch (e.g., 2.11.0), which is API-incompatible with the installed torch-npu (e.g., 2.2.0 or 2.5.1). This produces ImportError at import time: cannot import name 'FILE_LIKE' from torch.serialization. The issue only manifests at runtime, not during pip install.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Query the installed system torch-npu major.minor version (e.g., 2.5.1). 2. Pin torch to the matching major.minor version (torch==2.5.1). 3. Use --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu to resolve CPU-only torch wheels. 4. Pin torchvision to the matching version (e.g., 0.20.1+cpu for torch 2.5.1). 5. Run pip check after installation to catch version conflicts before runtime.
